feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: How the Ebola outbreak is testing U.S. pandemic preparedness
entry.summary: The crisis raises questions about whether the administration can implement a fast, coordinated response when many of the people and agencies that once drove those efforts are gone or diminished.

entry.title: People with eating disorders are taking GLP-1s and doctors are alarmed
entry.summary: Providers said they are encountering patients seeking treatment for anorexia, bulimia and binge eating disorder who are taking GLP-1 drugs.

entry.title: Apollo 12 was struck by lightning twice less than a minute after launch, and the mission kept going only because a flight controller recognised an obscure telemetry failure pattern and told the crew to flip a switch almost nobody else in the room understood
entry.summary: The core of this story holds up. On 14 November 1969, the Saturn V carrying Apollo 12 was struck by lightning twice in its first minute of flight, the spacecraft’s telemetry dissolved into nonsense, and a flight controller named John Aaron salvaged the situat…

entry.title: U.S. bans green-card holders from returning from Ebola-stricken countries
entry.summary: Health officials cite public health and « resource constraints » as they expand travel restrictions.

entry.title: Low-impact routine may help adults lower blood pressure at home
entry.summary: Conversation starters, celebrity health reveals, healthy living hacks and medical cautions: Here's what you missed in Fox News Health this week

entry.title: First Controlled Human Trial Linking Maitake Mushroom Consumption to Improved Cognitive Function
entry.summary: A rigorous 18-week clinical trial found that daily consumption of maitake mushrooms significantly improved cognitive scores in healthy older adults, particularly in memory.

entry.title: Ugandans rue link to Bundibugyo, the Ebola virus type named after a district of cocoa farmers
entry.summary: Before it became the somewhat easy-to-mispronounce name of a rare type of Ebola virus, Bundibugyo is a mountainous district in western Uganda that even some ...

entry.title: The Sweetener Trap: Are Sugar Alternatives Backfiring?
entry.summary: Emerging evidence suggests some sugar substitutes may affect metabolism, gut microbiome, and cardiovascular health more than previously recognized.

entry.title: CDC says green card holders who were recently in countries where Ebola is spreading can’t reenter U.S.
entry.summary: The move expands existing travel restrictions barring foreigners who’ve recently been in Congo, South Sudan and Uganda.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak now third largest recorded and "spreading rapidly"
entry.summary: Ebola outbreak risk level increased as deaths reach 177 with nearly 750 cases.

entry.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus on ship enjoy some Nebraska hospitality while waiting in quarantine
entry.summary: The 18 American passengers who were exposed to hantavirus on the MV Hondius cruise ship are getting a taste of Nebraska hospitality as they wait to find out ...

entry.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus on ship enjoy some Nebraska hospitality while waiting in quarantine
entry.summary: The 18 American passengers who were exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship are getting a taste of Nebraska hospitality as they wait to find out how much longer they will have to remain in quarantine at the hospital in Omaha. Elsewhere, a 12th illness linked t…

entry.title: Stress impairs your brain’s ability to link memories — dampening insight
entry.summary: Imaging suggests why the ability to make inferences declines after an episode of acute stress, such as a job interview.

entry.title: The One Vegetable Dietitians Recommend for Brain, Heart, and Gut Health
entry.summary: Not all vegetables are created equal. Two registered dietitians reveal their top pick for brain, heart, and gut health—plus a few honorable mentions worth adding to your rotation.

entry.title: No child deaths definitively linked to Covid shots, FDA says
entry.summary: No child deaths have been definitively linked to Covid vaccines, according to a report from the Food and Drug Administration that was quietly made public last week.

entry.title: This Teenager Can Revisit Her Memories So Clearly That Scientists Think She May Be Time Traveling
entry.summary: The Astounding Pop Mech Show examines a teenager with highly superior autobiographical memory—and why scientists think her case changes how we understand...

entry.title: Gas Station Cashier Refused to Sell a Pregnant Woman Cheetos She Had Walked Through the Rain to Buy — Reddit Is Outraged
entry.summary: The cashier refused to ring up the pregnant woman's Cheetos which she was craving and offered her a trail mix, instead.

entry.title: Gas Station Cashier Refused to Sell a Pregnant Woman Cheetos She Had Walked Through the Rain to Buy — Reddit Is Outraged
entry.summary: The cashier refused to ring up the pregnant woman's Cheetos which she was craving and offered her a trail mix, instead.

entry.title: 12 healthy habits that the gut scientist lives by
entry.summary: It’s not all about eating fermented foods. Microbiome expert Dr Emily Leeming on what else you can do to improve your health

entry.title: Hantavirus risk from rodents may be higher in Pacific Northwest
entry.summary: A hantavirus strain that causes deadly respiratory disease in humans may be more prevalent among rodents in Pacific Northwest, new study says.

entry.title: Landmark finding that showed brains of kids with ADHD mature later was actually a mirage in the data, new research finds
entry.summary: A "foundational" study found that the brains of children with ADHD matured later, but that finding was likely a mirage tied to issues with how the children...

entry.title: Updated trends in the global prevalence and burden of mental disorders, 1990-2023: a systematic analysis for the Global Burden of Disease Study 2023
entry.summary: Gates Foundation, Queensland Health, and University of Queensland.

entry.title: Does standing on a vibrating plate really burn fat and strengthen muscles?
entry.summary: Advocates sing the praises of whole-body vibration training. The science around it paints a more nuanced picture.

entry.title: Ebola Outbreak Death Toll Rises To 177: ‘Deeply Worrisome,’ WHO Chief Says (Live Updates)
entry.summary: One American who contracted Ebola is receiving treatment in Germany.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Bones When You Take Vitamin D and Calcium Together
entry.summary: Nutrition experts explain why taking vitamin D and calcium together may support stronger bones when it comes to bone health.

entry.title: U.S. doctor with Ebola feared he "wasn't going to make it" before evacuation from Congo
entry.summary: Dr. Peter Stafford was working with the missionary group Serge in Congo when he was infected with Ebola.

entry.title: Researchers Find Surprising Link Between Grapes and Sun Protection
entry.summary: Skin samples from people who ate grapes revealed increased natural protection against ultraviolet rays.

entry.title: Almost 50% of Preventable Cancers Linked to Just Two Lifestyle Habits
entry.summary: It's easy to feel powerless against cancer, but research has identified several ways that we can reduce the odds of it occurring.

entry.title: 4 Breakfast Foods with More Protein Than an Egg
entry.summary: Learn four breakfast foods that pack more protein per serving than an egg, with easy ways to add them to your morning routine.

entry.title: WHO chief says Ebola outbreak in Congo is 'spreading rapidly' and upgrades risk assessment
entry.summary: The head of the World Health Organization says the Ebola outbreak in Congo is spreading rapidly and poses a “very high” risk at the national level. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus announced the U.N. health agency has revised its assessment of …

entry.title: Lilly and Novo Weight-Loss Drugs Show Potential Cancer Benefits
entry.summary: The findings could broaden the long-term opportunity for obesity and diabetes treatments.

entry.title: Scientists discover simple way to relieve arthritis pain without pills or surgery
entry.summary: A surprisingly simple walking tweak may offer new hope for millions living with knee osteoarthritis. In a year-long clinical trial, researchers found that slightly changing the angle of a person’s foot while walking reduced knee pain as effectively as common …

entry.title: Weight-loss drugs from Lilly, Novo linked to stalling cancer: report
entry.summary: New studies link GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic and Mounjaro to lower cancer progression and death risk, including breast cancer benefits—learn what the data...

entry.title: It’s not always clear when someone’s lost the ability to decide
entry.summary: The complex changes of dementia and legal concepts like ‘lucid intervals’ show why patients and families must plan ahead

entry.title: Widely criticized keto diet study retracted
entry.summary: Aamulya/iStock A 2025 paper claiming the keto diet does not promote the formation of arterial plaques has been retracted after widespread criticism of the study’s methods and claims. The journal fo…

entry.title: Inside an African lab that helped crack the hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: (Clarifies status of Istitut Pasteur de Dakar in paragraphs 6, 8-9) By Jessica Donati DAKAR, May 22 (Reuters) - The call for help came in early May: a cruise...

entry.title: Plant-Forward Diets May Help With Menopause Weight Gain
entry.summary: A new study found that diets rich in plant foods and low in processed meats and salt may help with weight gain during menopause.

entry.title: The Type of Alcohol You Drink Could Affect How Long You Live
entry.summary: Researchers analyzing more than 340,000 adults uncovered an intriguing pattern linking alcohol type with long-term health outcomes.

entry.title: Air France flight to US diverted to Montreal due to Ebola travel restrictions
entry.summary: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said an Air France flight bound for Detroit was diverted to Montreal after a passenger from the Congo boarded “in error” amid flight restrictions tied to the Ebola outbreak

entry.title: The Surprising Sign You’re Experiencing Cognitive Decline, According to a New Study
entry.summary: A new study finds reduced light physical activity may signal early cognitive decline in older adults. Learn how brain health links to movement patterns.

entry.title: Spinach-Based Eye Drops Could Help Treat a Common Eye Condition
entry.summary: Plants rely on sunlight and photosynthesis to live, and now scientists have adapted the same process for the human eye – potentially unlocking new treatment options for dry eye syndrome, a discomforting condition affecting more than a billion people.

entry.title: Measles infections detected at 2 Heber City schools as Utah cases continue to rise
entry.summary: Health officials urge caution as measles infections hit two schools in Heber City.

entry.title: Two Legionnaires’ Cases Found at East Village Complex
entry.summary: City health officials advised tenants at Haven Plaza in Manhattan to stop taking showers and take other precautionary measures.

entry.title: Nearly 1.2 billion people worldwide are living with mental disorders. The number has been growing
entry.summary: Nearly 1.2 billion people had a mental disorder in 2023, a new study has found — reflecting a 95.5% increase since 1990.

entry.title: Air France flight to Detroit diverted to Canada under Ebola restrictions
entry.summary: A passenger from Congo should not have been aboard, authorities said. Under new DHS rules, people who recently visited any of three African countries must enter the U.S. via Dulles.

entry.title: De novo design of miniproteins targeting GPCRs
entry.summary: Nature - De novo design of miniproteins targeting GPCRs

entry.title: Experts Reveal the Symptoms of Hantavirus You Should Watch For
entry.summary: Find out what they are and how to stay safe.

entry.title: GLP-1 drugs may reduce the risk of cancer progressing, study suggests
entry.summary: GLP-1 drugs may be linked to a lower risk of cancer progression, according to new research that will be presented next week at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting.

entry.title: GLP-1s Linked to Lower Risk of Cancer Spread in Four Tumor Types
entry.summary: Accumulating evidence of possible benefit warrants randomized trials, experts say

entry.title: Machine Learning Doubles Depression Remission Rate
entry.summary: Machine-learning iMAP coaching track achieves a 55% depression remission rate by personalizing lifestyle targets.
